data.title: A Look At Doral Group Renewable Energy Resources (TASE:DORL) Valuation After New Off Grid Hydrogen Partnership In Spain
data.description:
H2Pro’s new agreement with Doral Group Renewable Energy Resources (TASE:DORL), through Doral Hydrogen, centers on an off grid, solar powered hydrogen project in Extremadura, Spain, linking directly into existing gas infrastructure.

See our latest analysis for Doral Group Renewable Energy Resources.

The new hydrogen partnership lands after a sharp run in Doral Group Renewable Energy Resources’ shares, with a 90 day share price return of 104.57% and a very large 1 year total shareholder return. However, the 7 day share price return of 5.48% suggests some recent cooling after strong momentum.

If this kind of green energy story has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan our screener of 23 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks as a starting point for other grid linked opportunities.

After that very large 1 year total return and with Doral still reporting a ₪348.115 million loss on ₪455.094 million of revenue, you have to ask yourself: is there real value left here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Preferred Price-to-Sales of 26.6x: Is It Justified?

Doral’s share price has surged over the past year, and at a last close of ₪59.12 the company is trading on a P/S ratio of 26.6x. That stands out against both its regional peers and the broader renewable energy industry.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value with its revenue, so a higher multiple usually reflects high expectations for future sales or profitability. For a business that is currently loss making, like Doral with a ₪348.115 million loss on ₪455.094 million of revenue, a rich P/S often means investors are placing a lot of weight on what the company might achieve rather than what it is earning today.

In this context, Doral’s 26.6x P/S looks expensive compared to the Asian renewable energy industry average of 2.4x, and it is also above the peer average of 16x. That is a wide gap and it suggests the market is assigning a premium to Doral relative to many other renewable names that generate similar types of revenue, even though Doral remains unprofitable and its losses have grown at 53.1% per year over the past 5 years.

data.title: A Look At AbraSilver Resource (TSX:ABRA) Valuation After Diablillos RIGI Approval
data.description:
AbraSilver Resource (TSX:ABRA) is back in focus after Argentina approved its Diablillos silver gold project for inclusion under the federal Large Investment Incentive Regime, or RIGI, a program offering long term fiscal and tax benefits.

See our latest analysis for AbraSilver Resource.

At a CA$14.20 share price, AbraSilver’s recent 1 day and 7 day share price declines sit against a much stronger 90 day share price return of 35.11% and a very large 1 year total shareholder return of 310.40%. This suggests momentum has cooled in the short term, while longer term sentiment has shifted markedly as the Diablillos project advances under RIGI and moves toward key permitting and feasibility milestones.

If this RIGI approval has you looking across precious metals, it could be a good moment to scan 7 top silver producer stocks as another way to spot opportunities in the silver space.

With the shares at CA$14.20, a very large 1 year return, a 30 day pullback, and a price target of CA$18.25 in view, the key question is whether AbraSilver is still mispriced or if the market already reflects future growth.

Advertisement

Preferred Price to Book of 44.5x: Is It Justified?

AbraSilver is trading on a P/B of 44.5x compared to a peer average of 8.9x and a Canadian Metals and Mining industry average of 3.6x. This points to a rich valuation against assets at the current CA$14.20 share price.

P/B compares a company’s market value to its book value. A higher ratio usually means investors are willing to pay more for each dollar of net assets, often when expectations for future project value are high. For AbraSilver, this elevated multiple sits alongside no meaningful revenue today and ongoing losses, so the market appears to be placing a lot of weight on the Diablillos project and future optionality rather than current financials.

Against both the 8.9x peer average and the 3.6x wider Canadian Metals and Mining benchmark, AbraSilver’s 44.5x P/B stands out as significantly higher. This suggests the market is assigning a substantial premium to its project pipeline and RIGI support compared with more mature or diversified producers.

data.title: Sanlorenzo (BIT:SL) Margins Hold Near 10% TTM As Growth Narrative Faces Slower Reality
data.description:
Sanlorenzo (BIT:SL) has released its FY 2025 numbers with third quarter revenue of €249.5 million and basic EPS of €0.83, backed by trailing twelve month revenue of €1.02b and EPS of €3.01. The company has seen quarterly revenue range from €230.4 million to €283.7 million over the last six reported quarters, with basic EPS between €0.61 and €0.86, while earnings over the past year are reported to have grown 7.4% and net profit margins sit at 10.4%. Overall, the results point to consistent profitability, but slightly softer margins, which sets up an interesting backdrop for how investors judge the quality and resilience of the business from here.

See our full analysis for Sanlorenzo.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results measure up against the prevailing narratives around Sanlorenzo, highlighting where the story is supported by the numbers and where it may need a rethink.

See what the community is saying about Sanlorenzo

Advertisement

TTM earnings growth slows vs 5 year pace

  • Over the last 12 months, earnings grew 7.4%, compared with a 21.4% annualized earnings growth rate over the past five years, and trailing EPS sits at €3.01 on €1.02b of revenue.
  • Consensus narrative talks about acquisitions and new products supporting long term revenue growth, yet current reported growth is more modest. Investors may compare this to:
    • Forecast revenue growth of 2.8% per year and earnings growth of 2.5% per year, both below the referenced Italian market forecasts of 5.5% and 10.3% respectively.
    • Five year annualized earnings growth of 21.4%, which is used to support the bullish idea that the business has grown well historically, even if the latest 7.4% pace is more restrained.

Margins steady around 10% despite softer mix

  • Net profit margin over the trailing 12 months is 10.4%, close to last year’s 10.7%, with trailing net income of €106.1 million on €1.02b of revenue.
  • Supporters of the bullish story point to capacity expansion and new vessels as margin drivers, and the current figures partly align with that idea:
    • The consensus narrative expects profit margins to move from about 10.3% to 10.8% over three years, so the present 10.4% is already around that starting point.
    • At the same time, flat guidance for 2025 in the narrative sits next to this stable 10% margin level, which may lead bulls to ask whether margin gains, rather than stronger revenue growth, do more of the heavy lifting.
On that margin story, if you want to see how optimistic investors frame the upside case, it is worth reading the full bull argument for Sanlorenzo: 🐂 Sanlorenzo Bull Case

Low 10.2x P/E against slower growth and dividend risk

  • The shares trade at €30.65 with a P/E of 10.2x, compared with a European Leisure industry average of 24x and a peer average of 21.5x. The reported DCF fair value of €50.32 and a 3.26% dividend yield highlight both an apparent discount and questions around free cash flow coverage for that dividend.
  • Bears focus on slower forecast growth and dividend coverage, and the current numbers provide some support for that cautious view:
    • Forecast revenue and earnings growth of 2.8% and 2.5% per year trail the referenced Italian market forecasts of 5.5% and 10.3%, which critics see as a reason the 10.2x P/E might remain below peers.
    • The dividend yield of 3.26% is flagged as not well covered by free cash flow, so investors who note the low P/E and DCF fair value of €50.32 may still want to check how much of the payout is backed by cash generation.
If you are weighing that lower P/E against these growth and dividend concerns, it can help to read a more cautious take on the story too: 🐻 Sanlorenzo Bear Case

data.title: Jeffrey Stafeil Joins Graphic Packaging Board As Leverage Scrutiny Rises
data.description:
• Graphic Packaging Holding (NYSE:GPK) has appointed Jeffrey Stafeil to its Board of Directors.
  • Stafeil brings extensive leadership experience from multinational industrial and manufacturing companies.
  • The board change is described as a material corporate governance development for the company.

Graphic Packaging Holding, which focuses on packaging solutions for consumer brands, is adding Stafeil at a time when packaging companies are closely watched for how they manage costs, supply chains and sustainability expectations. For investors, board composition can matter because it helps shape how management priorities are set and how major capital and operational decisions are reviewed.

Stafeil’s background in large scale industrial and manufacturing businesses may influence how NYSE:GPK evaluates efficiency, capital allocation and risk oversight. Investors who track corporate governance may want to follow future board level decisions, including how the company approaches long term planning and operational execution with this new voice in the room.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Graphic Packaging Holding by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Graphic Packaging Holding.

Does the team leading Graphic Packaging Holding have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

For you as an investor, Jeffrey Stafeil’s arrival on the Graphic Packaging board sits alongside recent decisions on leverage, dividends and capital allocation. The company has just secured more headroom on its credit agreement while it works through an aggressive inventory reduction plan and accepted tighter limits on share repurchases and certain acquisitions during that period. Bringing in a director with deep industrial and manufacturing experience could influence how the board weighs balance sheet flexibility, cash flow priorities and operational efficiency at a time when packaging peers like WestRock, International Paper and Amcor are also tightly focused on cost control and capital discipline.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Graphic Packaging Holding Narrative

  • Stafeil’s operational and manufacturing background could support the existing narrative focus on efficiency gains, margin mix and execution on large capital projects such as recycled paperboard investments.
  • His experience with leverage, covenant management and restructuring at industrial companies may lead to tougher scrutiny on debt levels and returns from major projects, which could challenge parts of the story that might otherwise appear overly optimistic.
  • The recent credit agreement amendment, with higher leverage covenants and limits on buybacks and acquisitions, is a fresh development that may not be fully reflected in older narrative assumptions about balance sheet strength and capital deployment.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Graphic Packaging Holding to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Debt has been flagged as not well covered by operating cash flow, and the higher leverage covenant through 2027 increases the importance of execution on cash generation.
  • ⚠️ Earnings are forecast to decline by an average of 1.5% per year for the next 3 years, which can limit flexibility if operating conditions or capital projects do not progress as expected.
  • 🎁 Trading at good value compared to peers and the broader industry may appeal to investors who are comfortable with balance sheet and earnings risks.
  • 🎁 The shares are described as trading at 58.6% below one estimate of fair value, which some investors may view as a potential upside if the company delivers on its operational and governance plans.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how Stafeil contributes to board level oversight of leverage, capital spending and inventory actions, and whether management updates its long term plans or financial targets in response. You can also monitor how the board balances cash returns to shareholders, within the US$65m annual repurchase cap, against debt reduction and future investment in recycled and fiber based packaging. Any changes in guidance, covenant usage or governance commentary will help you judge whether this board appointment is translating into clearer priorities and execution.

data.title: Stellantis Reset Charge And US Investment Reframe Electrification And Valuation Narrative
data.description:
• Stellantis recorded a €22.2b charge tied to a major reset of its long term plans.
  • The company is moving away from an all electric focus toward a broader mix that includes hybrids and internal combustion engines.
  • Management outlined a US$13b investment program in the US, with an emphasis on North American manufacturing and jobs.
  • The charge contributed to one of Stellantis’s largest net losses and marks a clear change in financial priorities.

For investors tracking Stellantis, ticker BIT:STLAM, this move lands at a time when the share price sits at about €5.974. The stock has retreated 38.5% year to date and 42.2% over the past year, continuing a multi year slide that has seen a 51.9% decline over three years and 44.0% over five years.

This reset on electrification and the large US investment outline a different capital allocation path. It could influence how you think about Stellantis in a global auto portfolio. The scale of the charge, the shift back toward hybrids and internal combustion engines, and the focus on North America are all likely to be important reference points as you assess future updates from management.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Stellantis by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Stellantis.

3 things going right for Stellantis that this headline doesn't cover.

The reset charge, shift away from an all electric focus, and US$13b US investment plan together point to Stellantis trying to realign its business model around where it currently sees the strongest demand and returns. The €22.2b charge is large enough to reshape reported earnings for 2025, which will matter if you are tracking valuation multiples. At the same time, the plan leans heavily into multi energy products like the Charger lineup and refreshed Pacifica, backed by new leadership moves at Chrysler and an expanded role for the Dodge CEO. For investors, this raises questions about execution risk in North America, how quickly the new hybrid and internal combustion heavy plan translates into better margins, and whether the updated model mix can support pricing against peers such as Ford and General Motors, as well as European groups like Volkswagen. The new hybrid bond issuance adds another layer, giving Stellantis more flexibility on liquidity as it absorbs the reset costs.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Stellantis Narrative

  • The pivot toward a multi energy lineup and refreshed US nameplates is consistent with the narrative that product launches and brand work in regions like North America can support future revenue growth and earnings power.
  • The large reset charge, loss for 2025, and references to restructuring and weaker profitability on electric vehicles underline the narrative risk that margin pressure and execution issues could weigh on long term earnings quality.
  • The decision to scale back some battery joint ventures and review electrification commitments looks like a new element that may not be fully captured in earlier expectations built around a heavier electric vehicle rollout.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Stellantis to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The highly volatile share price over the past three months, combined with one of the company’s largest reported losses, could lead to sharp reactions to future guidance changes or further resets.

data.title: GN Store Nord AGM Puts Cash Retention And Buyback Flexibility In Focus
